content.content:
I couldn't find an event with the exact title "Dental check-up" in your calendar. I did find two related events — here are the matches:

- Event id: 5
  - Title: Dentist Appointment
  - When: 2024-05-18 09:00 — 2024-05-18 10:00
  - Location: Downtown Dental Clinic
  - Participants: emma.johnson@bluesparrowtech.com
  - Description: Regular dental check-up.

- Event id: 23
  - Title: Moved: Clientcorp Presentation
  - When: 2024-05-17 14:00 — 2024-05-17 15:30
  - Participants: emma.johnson@bluesparrowtech.com, john.doe@clientcorp.com, maria.garcia@bluesparrowtech.com
  - Note: description mentions "Needed to move because I have a dental check-up."

Which should I reschedule? Based on your request, I think you mean the first one (id 5 — "Dentist Appointment") because its description explicitly says "Regular dental check-up."

If you confirm you want id 5 rescheduled, I will:
- Keep the original duration (1 hour) and move it to start 2024-05-20 10:00 (so end 2024-05-20 11:00).
- Notify you (emma.johnson@bluesparrowtech.com) automatically via the calendar update.
- Also notify the clinic — I need the clinic's email address to do that. Two options for notifying the clinic:
  1) Add the clinic email as a participant on the event before rescheduling — the calendar reschedule will email all participants.
  2) If you prefer not to change participants, I can send a separate email to the clinic notifying them of the new time.

Please confirm:
1) That I should reschedule event id 5 (Dentist Appointment) to 2024-05-20 10:00 — 11:00.
2) The clinic email address to notify (or tell me if you want a separate custom email text).
3) Whether to add the clinic as a participant (so the calendar reschedule email notifies them) or to send a separate email.

Once you confirm these, I’ll proceed.
